title: Enigma Stage 5 Fulldata Postmortem — 1500-Shard Validation on 8xH100
objective: Validate Stage 5 findings on 1500 data shards (~23% of ClimbMix-400B) to eliminate the data-recycling confounder from the 3-shard Stage 5 runs. Run the same 10 mutations from Stage 5 primary batch at 20k steps with full data diversity.

infrastructure.model: NanoChat GPT-2 (12 layers, 768 dim, 6 heads, ~124M params)
infrastructure.dataset: karpathy/climbmix-400b-shuffle, 1500 train shards + 1 val shard (~138B tokens available)
infrastructure.tokens_processed_per_run: 20k steps × 1024 tokens = ~20M tokens (<0.015% of available data, zero recycling)
infrastructure.total_wall_time: ~14 minutes for all 10 runs in parallel
infrastructure.optimizer: Production MuonAdamW with production schedules (lr warmdown, momentum warmup, wd decay)

key_difference_from_stage5.data_recycling: Zero recycling (20M tokens from 138B available) vs heavy recycling (20M from 180M) in Stage 5
key_difference_from_stage5.compute: Same model, same batch size, same steps, same mutations — only data volume changed
key_difference_from_stage5.gpu: H100 80GB vs B200 183GB — no functional difference at this batch size

key_ranking_changes: H64_H60_H73: #1 → #3 (previous champion falls, compound penalty at full data)
comparison_to_stage5_3shard.absolute_bpb_shift: All BPBs are ~0.25-0.33 higher on 1500 shards. This is expected — diverse data is harder to learn from in 20k steps with tiny batch. Relative rankings are what matter.

h73_dominance.finding: H73 (eps schedule) is the strongest single mutation, with 3x the delta of the next best compound
h73_dominance.mechanism: Production uses eps=1e-10 from step 0. With zero-initialized second moments, this means enormous effective step sizes early on low-variance dimensions. H73 starts at eps=1e-6 (1000x higher), providing implicit per-coordinate clipping during the noisy early phase, then anneals to the same 1e-10 production value.
h73_dominance.why_stronger_at_full_data: With diverse data (zero recycling), early gradients have higher variance across dimensions. The eps clipping is more valuable when the variance landscape is heterogeneous. On 3 shards with recycling, the variance landscape was artificially smoothed.

compound_collapse.finding: All compounds underperform H73 solo. The 3-shard compound wins were artifacts of data recycling.
compound_collapse.explanation: H64 (Nesterov blend) and H60 (beta2 warmup) were tuned to help in the low-data regime where gradients are noisy from repetition. With diverse data, these interventions add noise rather than helping. H73's mechanism is fundamentally different — it addresses a property of the optimizer initialization, not the data regime.

h71_confirmed_harmful.finding: Every compound containing H71 is worse than its H71-free counterpart. The full 4-way is the worst overall.
h71_confirmed_harmful.consistent_with: Stage 5 (3-shard) already showed H71 hurting in compounds. Now confirmed at scale.

h60_modest_but_real.finding: H60 solo = +0.019 BPB (#6). Real but 5x smaller than H73.
h60_modest_but_real.note: H60 was predicted to hold. It does, but the effect is modest. Beta2 warmup is a reasonable idea but eps scheduling is a much bigger lever.

predictions_from_checkpoint.result: +0.030 only in compound, not solo tested

caveats: 1500 of 6543 shards (~23% of full dataset). Data distribution may not fully represent the complete ClimbMix-400B.
Small batch (1024 tokens). Production uses 524288 tokens/step with different gradient noise characteristics.
Small model (d12, ~124M params). Optimizer dynamics change at larger scales.
Single GPU per run. Production uses 8-GPU DistMuonAdamW, though at batch=1024 this should be equivalent.
next_steps: Validate H73 at production batch size (524288 tokens) with DistMuonAdamW on 8xH100
Integrate H73 into base_train.py for Time-to-GPT-2 speedrun (target: beat 1.65hr record on 8xH100)
Do NOT compound H73 with H64/H60/H71 — solo is cleaner and stronger
